name: DarkPool DEX
description: Privacy-preserving decentralized exchange using zero-knowledge proofs for hidden order books. Trade without revealing your strategy.
readme:
## Overview

DarkPool DEX is a privacy-preserving decentralized exchange that uses zero-knowledge proofs to enable hidden order books. Traders can place orders without revealing their strategy, size, or timing to other market participants.

## Architecture

- **Order Commitment**: Orders are submitted as ZK commitments — the order details are hashed and the proof is submitted on-chain
- **Matching Engine**: Off-chain matching with on-chain settlement using PLONK proofs
- **Privacy Guarantees**: Order size, price, and direction are never revealed until execution

## Key Features

- Hidden order books with ZK proof verification
- MEV-resistant trade execution
- Cross-chain liquidity aggregation
- Gas-optimized PLONK verifier contracts

## Roadmap

- [x] Core ZK circuit design
- [x] PLONK prover implementation
- [ ] Testnet deployment
- [ ] Liquidity bootstrapping
- [ ] Mainnet launch

name: Whisper Protocol
description: End-to-end encrypted messaging protocol built on decentralized infrastructure. No metadata leaks, no central authority.
readme:
## Overview

Whisper Protocol is an end-to-end encrypted messaging system built on decentralized infrastructure. Unlike traditional messaging apps, Whisper has no central server — messages are routed through a peer-to-peer network with zero metadata exposure.

## Architecture

- **Transport Layer**: libp2p for peer discovery and message routing
- **Encryption**: Double Ratchet Algorithm (Signal Protocol) for forward secrecy
- **Identity**: Self-sovereign identities — no phone numbers, no email required

## Key Features

- Zero metadata leaks — message size, timing, and recipients are obfuscated
- Onion-routed messages through multiple relay nodes
- Offline message queuing with encrypted dead drops
- Group chats with sender anonymity

## Performance

- Message delivery: < 500ms (3-hop relay)
- Encryption overhead: < 2ms per message
- Network: 200+ active relay nodes

name: Phantom ID
description: Self-sovereign identity system with selective disclosure. Prove attributes about yourself without revealing your identity.
readme:
## Overview

Phantom ID is a self-sovereign identity system that lets users prove attributes about themselves without revealing their actual identity. Built on W3C DID and Verifiable Credentials standards with ZK proof integration.

## Use Cases

- Prove you're over 18 without revealing your birthday
- Prove you're a citizen without revealing your passport number
- Prove your credit score is above 700 without revealing the exact score

## Architecture

- **DID Method**: Custom DID method anchored to Ethereum
- **Credential Issuance**: Trusted issuers create signed credentials
- **ZK Proofs**: Circom circuits for selective attribute disclosure
- **Verification**: On-chain and off-chain verification paths

## Roadmap

- [x] DID method specification
- [x] Circom circuit library
- [ ] Mobile wallet app
- [ ] Issuer portal
- [ ] SDK for third-party integration

name: Synth Brain
description: Federated learning framework for training AI models across distributed nodes without sharing raw data.
readme:
## Overview

Synth Brain is a federated learning framework that enables training AI models across distributed nodes without sharing raw data. Each node trains locally and only shares encrypted model gradients.

## Design Goals

- **Privacy**: Raw data never leaves the node. Only encrypted gradients are shared.
- **Efficiency**: Compressed gradient updates reduce bandwidth by 90%
- **Security**: Secure aggregation prevents gradient inversion attacks

## Architecture

- **Coordinator**: Manages training rounds and aggregates updates
- **Nodes**: Local training with differential privacy
- **Communication**: gRPC with TLS for gradient exchange
- **Aggregation**: Secure multi-party computation for gradient averaging

## Status

Currently in concept phase. Research paper in progress.

name: OnChain Oracle
description: Decentralized oracle network for verifiable off-chain data feeds. Trustless price feeds for DeFi protocols.
readme:
## Overview

OnChain Oracle provides decentralized, verifiable off-chain data feeds for DeFi protocols. Built as a Chainlink node operator network with additional privacy features.

## Supported Feeds

- Crypto price feeds (50+ pairs)
- DeFi protocol TVL data
- Gas price predictions
- NFT floor prices

## Architecture

- **Node Network**: 12 independent oracle nodes with geographic distribution
- **Aggregation**: Median aggregation with outlier detection
- **Verification**: On-chain proof of data authenticity

## Stats

- 50+ price feeds live
- 99.9% uptime
- < 1 block latency
- Serving 15+ DeFi protocols

name: Stealth Scan
description: Privacy-focused blockchain explorer. View transactions without exposing your IP or linking queries to your wallet.
readme:
## Overview

Stealth Scan is a privacy-focused blockchain explorer that lets you view transactions without exposing your IP address or linking your queries to your wallet. All queries are routed through Tor.

## Privacy Features

- All queries routed through Tor network
- No query logging or analytics
- Local caching with IndexedDB — no server-side storage
- Randomized query patterns to prevent traffic analysis

## Design

- **Backend**: Go-based indexer running as a Tor hidden service
- **Frontend**: Static SPA with client-side rendering
- **Storage**: IndexedDB for local query cache and bookmarks

## Status

Concept phase. Architecture design complete.

name: CipherFS
description: Encrypted file system layer with plausible deniability. Hidden volumes, secure deletion, and zero-knowledge access control.
readme:
## Overview

CipherFS is an encrypted file system layer that provides plausible deniability through hidden volumes. Built as a FUSE filesystem in Rust with AES-256-GCM encryption.

## Key Features

- **Hidden Volumes**: Multiple encryption layers — reveal a decoy volume under duress
- **Secure Deletion**: Cryptographic erasure — destroy the key, destroy the data
- **ZK Access Control**: Prove you have access without revealing which volume
- **Cross-Platform**: FUSE-based, works on Linux and macOS

## Architecture

- **Encryption**: AES-256-GCM with per-file nonces
- **Key Derivation**: Argon2id with hardware-bound salt
- **Storage**: Block-level encryption with randomized block placement

## Roadmap

- [x] Core FUSE implementation
- [x] AES-256-GCM encryption layer
- [ ] Hidden volume support
- [ ] ZK access control circuits
- [ ] GUI wrapper

name: TokenScope
description: Real-time token analytics dashboard with on-chain data aggregation. Whale alerts, liquidity tracking, and holder distribution.
readme:
## Overview

TokenScope is a real-time token analytics dashboard that aggregates on-chain data for comprehensive market intelligence. Track whale movements, liquidity changes, and holder distribution patterns.

## Features

- **Whale Alerts**: Real-time notifications for large token movements
- **Liquidity Tracking**: DEX liquidity pool depth and changes
- **Holder Distribution**: Gini coefficient and concentration metrics
- **Price Correlation**: Cross-token price correlation matrix

## Data Sources

- The Graph subgraphs for on-chain data
- DEX event logs for trade history
- Token transfer events for holder analysis

## Stats

- Tracking 500+ tokens
- 10,000+ daily active users
- < 30s data latency
- 8 chains supported

name: ProofNet
description: Decentralized marketplace for ZK proof generation. Outsource heavy computation to specialized provers while maintaining privacy.
readme:
## Overview

ProofNet is a decentralized marketplace for ZK proof generation. Developers can outsource computationally intensive proof generation to a network of specialized provers without revealing the underlying data.

## Architecture

- **Prover Network**: GPU-equipped nodes compete to generate proofs
- **Job Queue**: Encrypted proof requests with blind computation
- **Verification**: On-chain proof verification and payment settlement
- **Privacy**: Provers never see the witness data — only the circuit

## Economic Model

- Pay-per-proof pricing based on circuit complexity
- Prover reputation system with slashing for invalid proofs
- Stake-weighted job assignment

## Status

Concept phase. Whitepaper in draft.
